content.problemStatement: Given a Binary Tree, return the Vertical Order Traversal of it starting from the Leftmost level to the Rightmost level. If there are multiple nodes passing through a vertical line, then they should be printed as they appear in level order traversal of the tree.
constraints: 1 <= Number of nodes in the tree <= 10^5
content.inputFormat: The input is a binary tree represented as an array where the index represents the node's value and the value at that index represents the left and right child indices. The root of the tree is at index 0.
content.outputFormat: The output is a 2D array representing the vertical order traversal of the binary tree.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Vertical Levels from left to right:Level -2: [4],Level -1: [2],Level 0: [1, 10, 9, 6] (Overlapping nodes are added in their level order sequence),Level 1: [3],Level 2: [11],
Vertical Levels from left to right:Level -2: [2],Level -1: [7, 5],Level 0: [2, 6],Level 1: [5, 11, 4] (Overlapping nodes are added in their level order sequence),Level 2: [9].

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use a queue to perform a BFS traversal of the tree, keeping track of the vertical and level of each node. Use a map to store the nodes by vertical and level.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) due to sorting
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the nodes in the map
